title: Lazy Fibonacci Stream
code:
fibs :: [Integer]
fibs = 0 : 1 : zipWith (+) fibs (tail fibs)

takeWhileUnder :: Integer -> [Integer]
takeWhileUnder limit = takeWhile (< limit) fibs

main :: IO ()
main = print (takeWhileUnder 1000)
description: Defines the Fibonacci sequence as a self-referential infinite list. `zipWith (+)` pairs each element with the next, and laziness ensures only needed values are computed. A classic demonstration of Haskell's lazy evaluation.
